transcript.text: Hello.
How are you?
Doing well.
How are you, Bailey?
Good.
Actually, I'm not doing great.
I caught some flu and I was feeling really bad yesterday.
I am better today, but you'll hear that I'm like not in the best of shapes.
Oh, I'm sorry.
It's the season.
Yeah.
I know.
I caught one from my kids a couple weeks ago and I sounded like a frog on my calls for a week, just like really force.
Yeah, that's where I'm at right now.
Plus, just to add to the fun, I kind of, you know how you go to bed and then you wake up and you're kind of like tight in a muscle.
Like I screwed up my neck just for the fun of it as well.
So I can't even turn my neck.
So it's just great all over the place.

You know, I was just talking to one of our directors about that, how you, the difference between being 20 and being older is you get sleep injuries where you injure yourself just like sleeping and then you wake up.
Yeah.
That's exactly it.
I'm sorry.
It sounds like a rough week.
So I know I saw on Slack that I think everyone won't be joining, but Mariana, I think, will be joining.
And I wasn't sure if there was anyone else from your side that would be joining this week.
Atul is the other product marketing manager that usually attends.
I'm guessing he will join.
He's in the office.
I'm just not sure.
Alberto won't be joining.
Yes, I saw on Slack.
Okay.
Well, I just wanted to start by introducing myself.
It's very nice to meet you.
I am Bailey, and I know you've had a few engagement managers over the last few weeks.
So I am sure it's been a bit of a hectic experience on your end.
So I am sorry for that.
But I am happy to be joining your account.
your account?
Oh.
Hi.
I was just introducing myself.
Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you as well.
So I was just saying that I've been at GrowthX.

joined kind of over the summer, but before that, I've been working in content and content marketing for a long time, sometimes in-house on the brand side, sometimes in a more agency setting, mostly B2B SaaS.

So I'm sort of more accustomed to working in regulated industries, industries where you have to be, you know, very careful with how you position your content and what you say, you know, across security and insurance and spaces like that.
So I am happy to be joining your account.
I've done a deep dive into sort of the documentation and what you've produced so far.
And luckily, Mariana was here to catch me up on the content you've produced so far.
So you don't feel like you need.
Okay.
Okay.
To like go in and explain yourselves all over again.
But it's very nice to meet you all.
And I'm happy to be here.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Great to have you.
And I'm located in Vermont, just in case you're curious.
I'm from New York City originally, but I've been in Vermont for about the last five years or so.
So I'm on the East Coast and you all are Pacific time, right?
Yeah.
Okay.
All right.
Well, I will just jump in and share the agenda for today.
Yeah.
So today I mostly kind of wanted to take a bit of a pulse check from you.
Like I know it's been kind of a rocky experience offboarding from Strategy Sprint into sort of our more steady state team.
So I wanted to get a sense.
From you, you know, how things are going.

I think right now, really focusing on honing the content to capture the nuances of your messaging, you know, reducing your editing time for each article so that, you know, we can increase our velocity.
So I wanted to touch base with you and see kind of how we're doing so far.
I think we're, you know, we're improving.
So, yeah, I mean, from certain iterations early on to where we're at now, I think I feel good.
You know, we still have feedback that we've been providing, but I think Mariana is doing a really good job at capturing that.
And as she says, she's turning it into guidelines and all that.
So I feel pretty good about, you know, kind of where we're headed.
Okay.
That is great.
That is what we want to hear.
You know, I think once we sort of have that confidence from you, then we can kind of focus more.
More on process and, you know, reducing the time it takes to edit articles and get them published.
And then also we can take a look at, you know, your strategy and kind of any goals you have coming for 2026 to make sure we're sort of thinking ahead as well.
So thank you for sharing that walkthrough of your new CMS.
Mariana is taking a look at that and we'll also share it with our publishing ops specialist.
We can follow up if we have any questions.
In terms of just ops, I wanted to...
Let me ask about that just because that's something I wanted to cover here, which is, I guess, a tool is probably doing this.
But like, so what we're trying to do is to get you enabled so that you can publish on our behalf in Webflow.
Yep.
Okay.
And do you have it?
Like, does your team have prior experience with Webflow?
Yes, we have a lot of clients who are on Webflow.
Webflow.
So...
Okay.
So when, like, by when do we think you're going to be able to, I guess, take over publishing, or is that already done?

We already do the publishing for you, but in a different CMS, we're just migrating to, so we need to, like, watch the walkthrough, see the articles there, like, try to replicate what Atul is teaching us in the walkthrough, and then publish the first one.
So that should be pretty fast.
Okay.
We have, I guess, it's two articles ready for published, and we'll test the walkthrough with those and see how it goes.
And in case we have any trouble, we will reach out to Atul, especially, right, that can help us with that.
Sure.

I mean, you should probably, for the very first one, you know, I don't know, like, do the whole staging thing, and then just kind of, before actually getting published, just check with the tool, making sure that we all, on both sides, agree that everything looks good.
Then go ahead and, you know, just so that there's no surprises.
We'll do that.
Yeah, we can definitely do that.
And often, like, changing CMSs, it's often things like blog categories.
Like, there are sometimes smaller details that if they don't get covered in the walkthrough, those are generally the things we have questions about.
But Webflow, we have lots of clients who are on Webflow.
And so in terms of just general familiarity with the platform, we have it.
Okay.
Okay, so we'll follow up with you on Slack if there are any questions there.
And, yeah, we can stage and then tag you to take a look.
In terms of reporting cadence, I know that last week, Joe sort of did a dive into reporting sort of through November.
Going forward, I wanted to see usually what we do is sort of a monthly reporting cadence.
Where we'll kind of report on across your metrics.
Rex.
And sort of also LLM visibility and sort of do a more deep dive once a month.
I know it sounded like based on last week's call that you have sort of regular board meetings or sort of other stakeholders that you're reporting up to.
So I wanted to touch base and make sure that that cadence made sense for you.
And also if there were any kind of particular metrics that you wanted to focus on or that you were sort of storytelling around for stakeholders to make sure we kind of captured that.
Well, we do have the board meeting that is a quarterly occurrence.
So whenever that happens, we'll definitely need, you know, supporting and providing key metrics for that.
Okay.
Today is one.
So you can imagine that, you know, December and then every three bonds.
Okay.
So that's great to know.
So that's something, you know, we can do it.
Okay.

Just more of a deep dive reporting monthly, and then in the week or two leading up to your board meeting, you know, if there are any sort of particular concerns or questions or, you know, stories you want to present to the board, that's something we can help support from a reporting perspective as well.
So I'll make sure that's on our calendar.
As for whether we want to do monthly reporting, not sure.
I think that's more of an Alberto question.
Like, he's the one that kind of drives our, that I feel like he should weigh in on whether that's enough or he's going to want it to, you know, more often than that.
Okay, well, we can, I can touch base with him on that either on Slack or we can talk about it next week.
Okay, in terms of content, Marianne, wasn't sure if there was anything, any questions you had here or if you wanted to give an update on anything here for the team.
No, we'll, we'll finish.
A batch and leave it for your review with five articles, so including all those competitor comparison series.
And then we have 14 more ideas ready to develop in the upcoming two weeks, so we should be good until 2026.
And Bailey will send out preparing the next batch of ideas for approval.

So, and Bailey, I don't know if you want to talk about that, but that maybe Alberto would want to participate in that conversation as well, like of having, instead of having the draft for the article, like the way we propose the idea, have that in a field in the content OS, instead of having to approve the doc with the draft, and then go through the development process, and then to the approval process.
That might be slowing us down, so maybe if we could add the very same information, but in a field.
Bye.
Bye.
In the content OS, that would make our lives easier.
So I don't know if that makes a difference for you.
Yeah, I think it makes sense.
So I think the goal is, and I sort of had that on the list, is the briefs that we send to you to kind of approve the article topic itself.

If there was a way to kind of condense that and give you an overview just as a column in Airtable, so you were able to sort of look at it more quickly without feeling like you had to go into one more document and add feedback, I think it would speed up that process and make it a lot more streamlined and faster on your end.

I think especially as you feel like we have a better grasp of the nuances around your product, and you probably, the goal is that you don't need like a full article brief every time, but sort of a briefer overview.
you.
Um,
To review instead.
But I think it's up to you with whether you sort of have the confidence to feel like we're there yet.
I think we, I would wait.

This is something that Alberto specifically requested to try to avoid, you know, things where we were going into the article and then there were like massive updates to be made because we were seeing inconsistencies in terms of sections relative to the, to the overall theme of the blog and things like that.
And so it's fairly new for us to go and try and change it.
would, you know, I would keep it for a little longer until we really start seeing that the review cycles are really minor and then, and then at that point we can evaluate that.
Okay.
That makes sense.
So we'll just make a note to kind of revisit that after a few more rounds of content and see how you're feeling about that.
Um, my other question with your kind of the review system that you have on Airtable right now.
Yeah.
Just confirming sort of how you prefer those to be assigned out.
Do you, are you, I know in the past, I think you've been self-assigning, and then I think maybe Joe assigned some to you.
So I just wanted to make sure that I understood the process, what worked best for your team in terms of deciding who was reviewing kind of which topic and which article.
I mean, yeah, I think, I think for, yeah, I don't think we have a specific process right now.
Okay.
I think in general, we should err on, you know, assigning more to Atul and myself than Alberto, just based on, on the type of role.
So, you know, perhaps Alberto should review, you know, 20 to 25% of the blogs at most, and then the rest of those you can kind of balance between Atul and myself.
Okay.
But also the comparison articles that are in production right now, like this list over here, Wolves,
Spot, Scylla, Lumano, those, Alberto will definitely want to take a look at those.
That is true.
Yeah, there are some critical ones that I guess will behave differently.
Okay, that makes sense.
So I think Alberto, it sounded like he kind of closed out everything he's doing.
I think there are still 10 posts in there waiting on feedback from you all.
So once you have a chance to take a look, let us know and we can get those updated and in the queue for staging.
And I think that was everything I had on the agenda today.

Was there anything else you all wanted to talk about, either from a production standpoint or I know on the call last week there was some confusion just about roles and kind of who was doing what for your team on GrowthX.
So if you have any questions about anything or kind of any clarification that you need, I'm happy to kind of speak to that as well.
Thank you.
I think we're good for now.
Okay.
Okay.
In that case, then you can expect new content from us this week and also new topics to come probably next week to kind of get us set up for January.
Excellent.
All right.
Yeah.
And let us know, we're always on Slack, so let us know if you have any kind of questions in the meantime.
Thank you.
All right.
Bye.
Bye, team.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content production, reporting, and process improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=208.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Engagement Manager:** Bailey Seybolt is the new GrowthX contact, bringing B2B SaaS and regulated industry experience.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)
  - [**Process Improvements:** GrowthX will take over Webflow publishing, staging all articles for Atul's final review. The idea to replace detailed briefs with Airtable fields was deferred to prevent major rewrites.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [**Review Cadence:** Review assignments will be split between Mau (40%), Atul (40%), and Alberto (20%), with Alberto focusing on critical competitor articles.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=772.0)
  - [**Reporting Cadence:** A monthly deep-dive report is proposed, with ad-hoc support for quarterly board meetings. Alberto will approve the final cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)

## Topics

### New Engagement Manager

  - [Bailey Seybolt is the new GrowthX Engagement Manager.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)
  - [Background: B2B SaaS content marketing, with experience in regulated industries (security, insurance).](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=127.0)
  - [Onboarding: Bailey completed a deep dive into existing documentation and was briefed by Mariana.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=162.0)

### Content Production & Process

  - [**Feedback:** Content quality is improving; Mariana is effectively incorporating feedback into guidelines.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)
  - [**Publishing Handoff:**](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
      - [GrowthX will take over Webflow publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
      - [Process: Stage articles → Tag Atul for final review → Publish.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=361.0)
      - [Rationale: Staging prevents surprises and ensures alignment on minor details (e.g., blog categories).](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=405.0)
  - [**Briefing Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
      - [Proposal: Replace detailed briefs with topic overviews in Airtable fields to speed up approvals.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
      - [Decision: Deferred.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)
      - [Rationale: Alberto requested detailed briefs to prevent major rewrites, a recent issue. The team will revisit this after review cycles become consistently minor.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)
  - [**Review Cadence:**](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=772.0)
      - [**Standard:** Mau (40%) & Atul (40%).](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=807.0)
      - [**Alberto (20%):** Focus on critical competitor articles (e.g., Wolves, Spot, Scylla).](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=812.0)
      - [**Action:** 10 articles are awaiting feedback from Ambient.ai.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=853.0)

### Reporting Cadence

  - [**Proposal:** A monthly deep-dive report on metrics and LLM visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
  - [**Board Meeting Support:** Ad-hoc reporting support will be provided for quarterly board meetings.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)
  - [**Decision:** Pending Alberto's approval.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=560.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
      - [Stage the first two articles in Webflow for Atul's review.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=361.0)
      - [Send the next batch of 5 articles for review.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
      - [Send 14 new topic ideas for approval.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
      - [Contact Alberto to finalize the reporting cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=560.0)
  - [**Ambient.ai:**](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=853.0)
      - [Provide feedback on the 10 articles currently awaiting review.](https://fathom.video/share/_JRznNQFdyKuqPwqkzyYcKKXz3ZAqW5b?tab=summary&timestamp=853.0)

action_items.description: Watch Webflow CMS walkthrough; share w/ publishing ops; publish 2 articles via staging; tag Mau/Atul; ping Atul if issues
Schedule monthly reporting + pre-board deep-dives w/ Mau/Atul
Confirm reporting cadence w/ Alberto on Slack or next week's call
Send next batch of topic ideas to Mau/Atul for approval
Assign reviews per guidance: Alberto 20–25%; balance Atul/Mau; Alberto reviews comparisons
